CREATE TABLE logs (event VARCHAR, duration INT);
SELECT printf('Benchmark "%s" took %d seconds', event, duration) FROM logs;
CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE user_logs (user_id INT, log_id INT);
SELECT printf('User %s had logs: %s', name, GROUP_CONCAT(log_id)) FROM users INNER JOIN user_logs ON users.id = user_logs.user_id GROUP BY users.id;
CREATE TABLE performance (test VARCHAR, duration FLOAT, max_duration FLOAT);
INSERT INTO performance VALUES ('Load test 1', 120.5, 150.0), ('Load test 2', 95.75, 100.0);
SELECT printf('Test "%s" finished in %.2f seconds, exceeding max limit of %.2f seconds', test, duration, max_duration) FROM performance WHERE duration > max_duration;
CREATE TABLE metrics (metric_name VARCHAR, metric_value INT);
INSERT INTO metrics VALUES ('Requests per second', 100), ('Latency', 250);
SELECT printf('System has %d %s with a latency of %d ms', metric_value, metric_name, (SELECT AVG(duration) FROM logs)) FROM metrics;
CREATE TABLE sessions (session_id INT, user_id INT, start_time TIMESTAMP);
SELECT printf('Session %d of user %s started at %s', session_id, (SELECT name FROM users WHERE id = user_id), start_time) FROM sessions;
